title: Tesla Q4 2025 Vehicle Production, Deliveries and Deployments
content: AUSTIN, Texas — Tesla, Inc. today announced production and delivery numbers for Q4 2025 and full year 2025. In Q4, Tesla produced 459,445 vehicles and delivered 495,570 vehicles. For the full year 2025, Tesla produced 1.77 million vehicles and delivered 1.79 million vehicles, a 2% decline from 2024 deliveries. Model Y deliveries in 2025 included updated 'Juniper' variants with refreshed interior and new front fascia, launched in China in January 2025 and globally in Q2 2025. Tesla expects to launch new, more affordable models in the first half of 2026. Energy storage deployments in Q4 were 11.0 GWh, bringing the full-year total to 31.4 GWh. Supercharger network expanded to 65,000+ stalls at 6,900+ stations. Elon Musk, CEO, stated on the subsequent earnings call: 'We expect vehicle growth to return to 20-30% in 2026 driven by the new affordable model launches and continued FSD expansion. Energy is our fastest-growing and highest-margin business segment.' Tesla Services and Other revenue grew 29% in FY2025 to $11.4 billion, reflecting FSD subscription growth and insurance expansion.
